definition: Private credit NAV smoothing masks true risk through systematic return deferral. Autocorrelation of 0.5-0.7 implies roughly half of each period's true return is deferred. After Dimson beta correction, reported Sharpe ratios of 0.8-1.2 fall to 0.4-0.6 — comparable to public high yield but with less liquidity. Over $300B in semi-liquid vehicles carry structural liquidity mismatch.
evidence: Autocorrelation of 0.5-0.7 in private credit returns
Dimson (1979) and Getmansky-Lo-Makarov (2004) beta correction methodology is standard and uncontroversial
Reported Sharpe ratios of 0.8-1.2 fall to 0.4-0.6 after correction
$300B+ in semi-liquid vehicles with structural liquidity mismatch
Went essentially unchallenged across all four debates
open_questions: Is the illiquidity premium rational compensation consciously accepted for behavioral benefits (reduced mark-to-market volatility)?
Will eventual mark-to-market events in BDC/interval funds produce restatement-like repricing?
How much of the systematic overweighting claim survives if the illiquidity premium is rationally accepted?
